channel.title: Tre_brock
channel.description: I write about what I do, and what I like.

item.title: VPNs The facts and the lies
item.description: I'm sure you have seen lots of youtubers talk about VPNs. "protect your privacy online" "Military grade encryption" "Protect yourself from attackers on public networks" etc etc etc... But are these claims really true? Is it worth spending your money on a VPN? First, privacy from VPNs. You will hear 90% of the youtubers who talk about a VPN in a sponsor talk about privacy. Most of the people these ads are shown to the VPN to will practically nothing. There probably on a windows laptop in google chrome and logged into their favorite social media site. A VPN changing your IP address will not help you. A much better (and free) way to upgrade your privacy would be to switch to brave. This is the best out of the box privacy that will not disrupt someones workflow at all. There are better options but this is the easiest that also has good payoff. Second, influences will say how VPNs have "military grade encryption". Fun fact, all websites with the lock icon have that too! None of your credit card info is visible. None of your passwords are. Unless you're on a unencrypted website. (No lock, big warning before visiting). Your ISP and people trying to snoop on the network (say in public wifi) will be able to see you are going to randombank.org or myemail.net, but they will not be able to see anything else. And the newer versions of Firefox are trying to mitigate this. There is only 2 uses for a VPN, changing location for a streaming service, or breaking the law in some way. Might that be piracy, or getting passed blocking (but tor can do this, and with even more privacy!) So why are people promoting these if there isn't much purpose? $MONEY$, it's all about the money. VPN sponsors get you big money and people who sign up with your code gives the creator a kickback with every purchase.

item.title: Fixing my site for moblie
item.description: I do web browsing on my computer for like 90% of the time, so I never really tested my site on a phone. But when I did I noticed how bad it looked. The home page was not broken, just everything was very tiny. But the add-ons page was broken, with elements over lapping each other. The Feeds had the same problem as the home page. After looking for a bit online I found a solution to the scaling problem. Then after more looking I made it so the elements that overlap on the add-ons page will disappear if the screen is too small.

item.title: I made my website compatible on the Wii
item.description: Today I was using my Wii and decided to use the internet browser on my Wii. My website worked ok but there way some problems. First, the gradients on my website were not working. This was fine on the feed pages, but on the other pages the background was completely black. I fixed this by making the edges of the background the color I wanted, and it looked cool. I never tried to fix the Seamonkey add-ons store because there is no reason for it to work on the Wii. After I had fixed the gradient there were still other issues, one of them was the video in my using gmail on epyrus tutorial did not load, the Wii browser does not support webm video. Something I knew the Wii supported was flash. So I made it that if your browser can not render the video it asks you if you want to view it with flash player. All browsers now can view webm videos so I thought only really old browser that needed flash would not be able to render it. Another issue was that the Wii renders svg images wrong. It makes them way to small. I changed all the svgs on my website into pngs. The final issue on my website was that on the page covering the add-ons I have made, the links to my Firefox and Seamonkey add-ons profile were too long and got cut off by a div. I thought they were too long even before doing this so I shorted them. After all that my website works flawless on the Wii. Why did I do this? Because it was fun.

item.title: My Seamonkey add-ons store
item.description: The Seamonkey add-ons store is almost broken right now. Many of the themes are not compatible. But they actually are, they just need their install file updated. But this can't happen.The store no long accepts new add-ons or updates to add-ons. Many popular add-ons have long been removed from the site because if they updated on the Thunderbird add-ons store to support web extensions then they would have been removed from the Seamonkey store. Many add-ons are still being worked on by people (and me). But you have to go on many different websites to download each one. So I made a store to compile a bunch of popular add-ons, or ones that I have made/updated myself. I also have added a few complete themes, that are on the official store but are only made for old version of Seamonkey, so I updated them. Also I will be accepting add-on and theme submissions, so if you find or have made a cool add-on, feel free to submit it! (but it better be free software). You can find my add-on store on https://tre-brock.xyz/Seamonkey-addons/

item.title: A addon to view addons and edit preferences in xul based browsers
item.description: I love browsers like Seamonkey and Basilisk, but a problem I have is when you click the addons button, it just closes your tab and goes to about:addons. Modern Firefox lets you see your addons without leaving the current tab, and when you click the gear icon on the extention you can edit the preferences. So I made an addon to do this. When you click the extention (you might have to edit the toolbar and drag it there) it shows the about:addons page. You can disable and enable addons, remove them, or edit their preferences.

item.title: How to add a Gmail account to Epyrus email client
A slight redesign and upgrade for my website
item.description: I decided my website looked pretty boring. It was just grey background. So I made the background for things gradient and more colorful while still being dark. I also added a donation link on my addons page. By default the PayPal donation link tracks its users and reports to PayPal. So I removed the tracking pixel from my code and removed it needing to phone home to PayPal for the icon. It is now just stored on my website. The donation also included the first box on my website. It also is the first time I have put multiple things next to each other. I might start making my website less long and removing a lot of the unused space. Also on this website I changed the icon for the text "message me" to something that fit the background color more. I also moved the addons page higher because it is more important. On the contact page I added a signal icon that better contrast to the background. I also added the link to my IRC channel. Finally I made the images of Trisquel Gnu/Linux smaller because they are not as important as my actual work. That's it for my website cleanup.

item.title: Seamonkey internet suite review
item.description: The Seamonkey internet suite includes multiple programs in one. These are; browser, HTML editor, contact book, mail and RSS client, and a calendar. It uses the gecko rendering engine by Mozilla (also used by Firefox and Thunderbird). Seamonkey has a good ui, it has the different apps at the bottom of the browser and the bookmarks tabs and controls at the top. My one issue is that to close tabs you have to either right click a tab to close it or press the tab close button while you are on the tab you want to close. To fix this issue you can install. Seatab X 2 from the Seamonkey addon store. The Seamonkey browser is light on ram and is pretty fast. The mail client is smooth. I love how you can have many accounts at once and look through them all. Having your RSS feed with your mail is convenient too. Seamonkey also has a feature where it can detect RSS on a page. The HTML editor is good. It lets you resize images easily. You can edit the HTML page as if you are viewing it and not viewing code. It great to see it visually. But not being able to edit CSS is a bad spot, meaning you will need more then 1 editor. The IRC client of Seamonkey is actually the Firefox addons ChatZilla, and it works. Although it is an addon it feels like it was built for Seamonkey. The ui is basic but is fits Seamonkey. It works for being an irc client. It's speedy and clean. It also supports plugins itself and integrates into the Seamonkey settings. Finally I will be reviewing the calendar. It is built into the mail app, you get to it by clicking "Events and Tasks" at the top bar then clicking calendar. It's a pretty basic calendar app. It lets you use local calendars and use ones from the internet. It also lets you create tasks. It does what you think a calendar would do. It is also technically an addon called lightning. Overall Seamonkey is a great internet suite that I recommend you use. Ps: if you want a good ad and tracker blocker the look up ublock origin legacy and install that from GitHub.

item.title: I made a new browser addon
item.description: I made a new addon that lets you view github notifications and messages from cliking on the addon. Now you don't have to have a tab for checking on your messages. I don't use github that much anymore so it is nice not having to have it open for a notifaction here and there. The reason reaon this only supports Pale Moon and seamonkey is they support legacy addons format.

item.title: My Pizza Tower Review
item.description: Pizza Tower is one of the best games I have ever played. The music in this game is amazing, it is fast paced but does not feel life it is rushing you, and the sounds are also very good. The artwork in this game is amazing, at first it just looks like it was drawn in ms paint, but when you start playing you see that the animation is incredibly detailed and fluid. The art style is also amazing. But the best part of the game is the game play. There is so many different moves you can do in the game, but they all flow together perfectly. Almost every level does stop you from maintaining a super fast speed. If your reaction time is fast you can just keep mach running. Another part of the game is the ranks. They are from D-P (PSABCD). You can get a better rank by keeping your combo and getting secrets and the toppins. If you get all secrets all toppins and you keep you combo the whole time then you can get a P rank. Also there is a thing called lap 2. You play the level backwards a second time. You need to do this for a P rank. Recently the Devs who added a new playable character (he is also the third world boss). It is the noise. He looks simaler to the dominoes noid. The noise campaign is very different because his changed move set. Overall Pizza Tower is an amazing and repayable game that you should buy.

item.title: I added RSS to my website
item.description: I have watched a few videos on rss and read articles on and and have decided to add rss to my website, I left mastodon posting aout a month ago and havent posted on anything since. But now I will be posting on this rss feed!
